message:
fix(btree): restore cursor stack on empty child subtree during next() traversal

When advance_next_impl descends into a child subtree via
move_to_leftmost_leaf and the subtree is empty (all rows deleted), the
cursor stack is left in an inconsistent state — the stack frames pushed
during descent are not unwound, causing subsequent advance_next_impl
calls to either skip entries or loop infinitely.

Fix: clone the stack before descending and restore it if
move_to_leftmost_leaf returns false (no rows found). This ensures the
cursor resumes forward traversal from the correct parent position.

Also adds a bounds check for cell_idx >= cell_count on interior pages
(index B-trees). When the cursor is positioned past the last cell of
an interior page, it should pop up to the parent rather than attempting
to access a non-existent child pointer.

Tests:
- test_cursor_next_skips_empty_table_child_subtree_without_restarting_root:
  3-child interior root where the middle child is empty — verifies next()
  skips it and continues to the rightmost child
- test_cursor_next_handles_empty_rightmost_table_child_subtree:
  2-child interior root where the rightmost child is empty — verifies
  next() cleanly reaches EOF

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
